content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach consistently names how the client is showing up, not just what they say, and the reads precede inquiry. Turn 2 opens with a somatic read of the exhale and the exhaustion underneath. Turn 4 names the specific signature of 'louder after success' and refuses to move past it. Turn 6 catches the client's pre-emptive self-dismissal ('which I know is textbook impostor syndrome — almost like you're trying to dismiss it before I do'). Turn 8 reads the confessional framing of the client's self-description. Turn 10 delivers the key pattern read — the fast-processing/synthesizing-away-the-good signature — and names it operatively: 'you receive something, you process it fast... you're doing it with your own wins.' Turn 12 distinguishes 'that's not the critic, that's you' and pivots to a precise somatic inquiry ('where was it in your body'), which unlocks the bracing realization in turn 13. These are specific, AoA-style reads (rush/intellectualizing signature, the happiness filter / shutting down warmth, bracing, pre-emptive defense), timed at moments that change the conversation rather than landing after the fact.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens with somatic read of the exhale and names exhaustion underneath the content."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the specific signature — critic loudest after success — and explicitly slows down to not move past it."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the linguistic caveat: client pre-emptively dismissing with 'textbook impostor syndrome' before the coach can."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the confessional tone of the self-description ('you said that like it's a confession')."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pattern-level read of the happiness filter / fast-processing signature: receives, synthesizes, moves on — applied to the client's own wins. This is the read that shifts the conversation."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Distinguishes the said from the shown — 'that's not the critic, that's you' — and grounds the next question in a specific somatic inquiry."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names what just happened in real time: client asking from inside experience rather than from a framework."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-happiness-filter", "rehearsing-how-to-say-it"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "The 'don't get too comfortable' / bracing sequence is essentially a happiness-filter read made operative. The coach also reads the rehearsed/performed quality of the client's earlier responses (the 'I know IFS, I know ACT' deflections) and meets them directly."
}
